bead.title: Complete HELIX's artifact-type metadata across all artifact directories
bead.description:
HELIX's methodology defines a catalog of artifact types (Vision, PRD, FEAT, US, ADR, SD, TD, TP, etc.). Each artifact type ships type-level metadata (id, prefix, phase, output location and naming, prompt and template pointers, examples, id_format) that lets any substrate (the DDx tracker today, potentially others) register and reason about that artifact type.

Today the metadata is incomplete and inconsistent:
- Five artifact directories under workflows/phases/ have no meta.yml at all (product-vision, business-case, competitive-analysis, opportunity-canvas, implementation-plan) — they are invisible to any registry that walks meta.yml files.
- Fourteen dependencies.yaml files across artifact directories are home-rolled and parallel to meta.yml — pure cruft. The instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists; type-level dependency declarations are not consumed by any substrate today.
- Existing meta.yml files carry a dependencies: block that no consumer reads.

Scope:
1. Create meta.yml for every artifact missing one. Fields: artifact.{id, prefix, phase}, output.{location, naming}, id_format.{prefix, pattern, example}, prompts.{generation, review}, template.file, examples[].
2. Delete all dependencies.yaml files under workflows/phases/.
3. Strip the dependencies: block from every existing meta.yml.

HELIX defines this metadata as an open schema. DDx happens to consume a subset of it (see DDx's cli/internal/artifacttypes/loader.go), and the field set chosen here should be a strict superset of what any single substrate parses — so any compliant consumer works out of the box. No graph data is lost — the instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists.
bead.acceptance: Every workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/ directory contains meta.yml, template.md, and prompt.md. rg dependencies.yaml workflows/ returns no matches. No existing meta.yml carries a dependencies: block. The DDx artifact-types loader (one consumer) sees every HELIX artifact type; a substrate-neutral walk of the meta.yml inventory enumerates the same set.
